title: senna italica
intro: Senna italica, also known as Egyptian senna or Alexandrian senna, is a plant species that belongs to the pea family and is native to Africa and the Middle East. The plant's leaves and pods are used in herbal medicine, particularly for their laxative properties, which are due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides. The medicinal properties of Senna italica include stimulating bowel movements, reducing inflammation, and improving digestion. The health benefits of the plant include treating constipation, hemorrhoids, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or capsules for consumption.
